name: Walk
description: Controlled low-commitment ground locomotion that lets the player position precisely without entering a faster run, sprint, or evasive state.
design_notes: Walk is the baseline for readable locomotion. Define how input_vector becomes character motion, how acceleration and deceleration curves feel on different surface materials, how slope limits clamp movement, and which animation blend states own foot placement. It should be dependable enough for precision positioning, stealth pacing, and interaction alignment without stealing the role of run or sprint.
design_purpose: Give players precise positioning for exploration, stealth, platform setup, and interaction alignment.
Provide a predictable baseline for comparing faster or more committed locomotion states.
Support animation readability, camera framing, and low-pressure navigation through dense spaces.
player_fantasy: The player feels fully in control of the character, able to approach, line up, and stop exactly where intended.

parameters.name: walk_pace_units_per_second
parameters.description: Target ground velocity used by the walk locomotion state.

parameters.name: walk_acceleration_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve used when input_vector moves from idle into walking.

parameters.name: walk_deceleration_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve used when the player releases or reverses movement input.

parameters.name: walk_turn_degrees_per_second
parameters.description: Maximum facing rotation while grounded and not locked by animation.

parameters.name: walk_slope_limit_degrees
parameters.description: Steepest walkable ground angle before sliding or blocking movement.

parameters.name: walk_camera_profile_id
parameters.description: Camera follow profile used while the character is in walk state.

edge_cases: Tiny analog stick drift should not move character_id unless it passes the dead-zone policy.
Slope transitions need a consistent ground_contact_state when the character crosses edges or stairs.
Walk should choose a clear priority when aim lock, dialogue focus, or interact prompts also consume input_vector.
Networked walking needs client_input_sequence reconciliation without visible foot sliding after correction.
common_bugs: Movement uses render delta instead of fixed-step simulation and becomes inconsistent at unusual frame rates.
Acceleration resets every frame because input_vector is normalized after applying analog magnitude.
Animation blend thresholds do not match walk_pace_units_per_second, causing foot skating.
Ground detection sticks to steep collision and lets characters walk up surfaces beyond walk_slope_limit_degrees.
balancing_notes: Walk pace defines the scale of rooms, interaction spacing, patrol avoidance, and camera comfort.
If walk is too fast, run and sprint lose identity; if too slow, basic traversal feels padded.
High turn rates help accessibility and precision, but can make weighty characters feel frictionless.
accessibility_notes: Support analog input, remappable hold-to-walk, and optional toggle-to-walk for players who cannot hold a modifier.
Avoid tying precise walking to narrow stick angles without calibration and dead-zone settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Store movement_state_id as walk and resolve input_vector before FixedUpdate motion. Feed target velocity through CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody.MovePosition, apply slope checks from contact normals, and drive Animator blend parameters from the resolved walk pace rather than raw input.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep walk as an explicit locomotion_mode_id on CharacterBody2D or CharacterBody3D. In _physics_process, apply acceleration and deceleration curves to velocity, call move_and_slide with floor limits, and update AnimationTree blend values after collision resolution.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement walking mode with custom run data separated from animation. For multiplayer, send client_input_sequence and reconcile server corrections without letting animation root motion override correction frames.
implementation_notes.web: Represent walk state in the fixed simulation loop with input_vector, ground_contact_state, and surface_material_id. Resolve collision before render interpolation, then pass the final velocity into Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or a custom animation layer.

example_games.title: The Last of Us
example_games.note: Uses controlled walking for exploration, stealth approach, and close interaction spaces.

example_games.title: Resident Evil 2
example_games.note: Uses deliberate walking pace to support aiming, tension, and room navigation.
